summary: Break apart Typescript compilation and public type definition check CI tasks
description: Our Typescript CI tasks both attempt to compile the driver and to check the public types in the same task.  We should break these into two separate tasks, so we can control them independently and view the output of each task separately.

summary: negative 0 should serialize to Double
description:
h3. Use Case

As a BSON library user, 
I want -0 to serialize to the Double type
So that it remains negative when round-trips from BSON. 
h3. User Impact

*How severe is the impact? Is anyone blocked or broken?

Users who use -0 are not able to preserve the negative.
h3. Dependencies
 * JS-BSON, BSON-EXT

h3. Unknowns
 * Is it a breaking change to start serializing -0 differently?
 * How does BSON-EXT serialize -0?

h3. Acceptance Criteria
 * TBD, based on unknowns

h3. Implementation Requirements
 * to have bson.serialize(-0) to Double (BSON type 0x01)

h3. Testing Requirements
 * see js-bson/test/node/double_tests.js for skipped test __

summary: Use Object.keys to enumerate keys on a JS object passed to BSON
description:
h3. Description

Currently the BSON library uses a for-in loop to enumerate the keys in a JS object. This will also enumerate keys defined on the prototype in addition to the object itself.

h3. Reproduction

{code}
> Object.prototype.myKey = 3
3
> BSON.serialize({})
<Buffer 10 00 00 00 10 6d 79 4b 65 79 00 03 00 00 00 00>
> BSON.deserialize(BSON.serialize({}))
{ myKey: 3 }
{code}

h3. Qs
* Should this wait for the next major?
** Perhaps, but keys on the prototype being serialized is likely to be unexpected.
** However it does pose risk to those that are doing very legacy object prototype construction, should we flag it?

h3. AC
* Consider using Object.keys() which will only enumerate the keys defined on the object itself
* Call out the potential breakage risk that this change poses in the release notes.
* Make the same change to EJSON

h3. Test AC
* Test prototype properties are ignored

summary: Error on non-object inputs when encoding BSON
description:
DrPizza [points out|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/319] that we are presently unable to roundtrip single values through our javascript BSON library: 
* {{BSON.deserialize(BSON.serialize([]))}} yields "{}"
* {{BSON.deserialize(BSON.serialize([1, 2, 3]))}} yields "{0: 1, 1: 2, 2: 3}"
* {{BSON.deserialize(BSON.serialize(1))}} yields "{}"
* and so on..

We should consider two approaches, and perhaps a marriage of the two:
* Explicitly disallow non-object inputs
* Provide a mechanism to roundtrip single values (perhaps wrapping the value in some reserved key like {{$$single$$}}, we do something similar in Swift)

summary: Remove getNonce command usage for 6.2+
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2490}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Destroy MessageStream when connections are destroyed
description:
h3. Use Case

As a Node Engineer
I want to ensure that async resources are cleaned up
So that I can prevent memory leaks.

The {{Connection}} class does not destroy its message stream when cleaning itself up. This could lead to leaking memory, and can lead to bugs as the message stream can still produce values after the underlying raw socket has been closed.
h3. Unknowns
 * Should the {{Connection}} class null out the message stream during cleanup to allow for earlier gc?
 ** No, not necessary.
 * {{Connection}} has a destroyed flag and a closed flag. Which one is the one that means the connection will be cleaned up?
** (See Documentation Requirements)

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * Ensure that {{MessageStream}} (kMessageStream) is destroyed whenever associated TCP socket(kStream) is destroyed/ended

h4. Testing Requirements
 * Add a unit test to check that {{Connections}} destroy both the socket and the {{MessageStream}}
 * Add test that checks that {{MessageStream}} cannot produce values after TCP socket is closed
 * If worth doing, add test that checks that the {{MessageStream}} no longer exists after destroying the socket

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * Document difference between closed and destroyed flags in the doc comments (The time at which they are set)

summary: Not all errors from BSON extend from BSONError
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

Some of the errors were missed when we created a new BSONError class [here|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/pull/464]. See long.ts and serializer.ts

In addition, in order to not break inheritance a new BSONTypeError was created to inherit from TypeError. However, this creates a disconnect where some errors are instanceof BSONError and some are instanceof BSONTypeError.
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

BSON v4 and soon v5
h3. What is the improvement you would like?
 * Remove BSONTypeError, it would be confusing to keep it and have it subclass from BSONError
 * Either:
 ** -Do not add any subclasses to BSONError now or ever, instanceof is finicky in web environments, users that face cross realm issues would prefer to check {{error.name}}-
 ** Or consider adding a BSONError.isBSONError(error) à la Array.isArray to support filtering in places where instanceof does not work

h4. -Follow up if no subclasses-
 * -If an improvement to error subject area is desired consider an enum of strings prepended to the message: (subject or code)-
 * -{{new BSONError('cannot put null in keys', \{ subject: 'serializer' })}}-
 ** -{{Uncaught: BSONError: [serializer] cannot put null in keys}}-
 ** -{{error.subject === 'serializer'}}-

summary: memory leak when breaking in the middle of a cursor's async iterator
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

When using an async iterator, like
{code:java}
const cursor = col.find()

for await (const doc of query) {
  console.log(doc)
  break
}{code}
breaking the iterator before the end causes a memory leak. The 'sessions' used on the find are not closed/cleaned up
Printing activeSessions's size from client.s.activeSessions reveals that, when breaking in the middle of an async iterator (before the end), the number of sessions increases indefinitely, which does NOT happen if you consume the iterator thought the end (the number stays consistent)
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

mongodb driver for nodejs, latest version (4.9.0)

It seems that the bug was introduced on [#3286|[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/3286],] it was the last PR that changed the src/mongo_client.ts and introduced the activeSessions Set

I've tested it with a version before this commit (4.5.0), that uses the old sessions Set and the leak seems to not happen
h3. Steps to reproduce?

I've made a repository to reproduce this bug: [https://github.com/GCastilho/mongodb-cursor-memory-leak]

Just clone it, and run 'npm start'. The used data as well as the 'activeSessions' will be printed periodically for one minute

summary: Remove lastOp / opTime from bulk response
description:
h3. AC

- Remove lastOp from bulk operation responses - it is not a drivers spec feature and does not seem to provide value to our users,

- Document change in the migration guide
h3. What use case would this feature/improvement enable?

It would reduce the unnecessary maintenance burden and ensure that the other parts of bulk processing that *are* in the spec are more resilient.

summary: Use 1 instead of true for hello value
description:
We currently send {{hello: true}} to MongoDB which works fine, it would be nice to align with other drivers and send {{hello: 1}} / {{{}[LEGACY_HELLO]: 1{}}}. We discussed doing this in the major to be overly cautious about a change to commands sent from the driver. This should have no impact.

AC
 * make the change
 * Document change in the migration guide
 * add a regression test

Properly support react native in BSON v5 bundle exports
description:
We should continue to support react native in the next major version of BSON. All we need is to specify which bundle react native tooling should pull in.

h3. AC
* Add a "react-native" property to the package.json's "exports" field. Set it to point to the commonjs module.
* Add documentation to the readme describing which polyfills are required to make the library function properly.

h3. Follow up
* Add react native testing by running tests in [hermes|https://hermesengine.dev/] to BSON CI

Missing callback typings for insertOne method
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

It is not possible to use the insertOne method with a callback, because the typing in the mongo-legacy.d.ts is missing.
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

mongo-legacy 4.0.0
h3. Steps to reproduce?
this.db.collection(this.collection).insertOne(entity, (err, _) => cb(err, entity as T));

summary: Spec Compliance: BulkWrite result are not the same as spec expectations
description:
In the retryable spec tests we need to test partial deep equality with the expected result, in addition to checking first for a "value" property that holds the real operation result.

We should investigate what changes are needed to make these CRUD operations exactly deep equal to the spec expectations.

Test runner in:
{{retryable-writes/retryable_writes.spec.test.ts}}

 

AC:
 * Make the sure the following are accessible on the BulkWriteResult class:

 ** deletedCount
 ** insertedCount
 ** insertedIds
 ** matchedCount
 ** modifiedCount
 ** upsertedCount
 ** upsertedIds
 * Make the {{result}} property private and non-enumerable. (NODE-4393)
 * enable the deep equality checking in the test runner
 * Mark {{BulkResult}} as internal in the API.
 * Remove BulkWriteResult.toJSON()
 * Document changes in the migration guide
 *

summary: Change return type of AbstractCursor's async iterator to AsyncGenerator
description:
Currently, the async iterator in abstract cursor can return an {{AsyncGenerator}} when using native promises or an {{AsyncIterator}} when using 3rd-party promises. For the sake of consistency, we should change the return type to always be {{AsyncGenerator}} since it's a stricter type than {{{}AsyncIterator{}}}.

When we remove the custom promise version of the async generator we can update the return type to be AsyncGenerator.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
 * change the return type of the asyncIterator symbol in abstract cursor to be {{AsyncGenerator}}

summary: Cursor.clone does not return legacy version of cursor
Move AWS optional dependency to peerDependencies
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

mongodb v4.11.0 has an optional dependency which installs many packages which never get used if you don't use AWS.

yarn v3 does not support ignoring optional dependencies, so we have to install all the optional packages.

The change was introduced in https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-4721

Proposal: move the AWS optional dependency to peerDependencies
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

mongodb v4.11.0

yarn v3.2.4
h3. Steps to reproduce?

yarn install

summary: Update libmongocrypt to set aws/credentials as an optional peer dependency
description:
We want the aws credentials provider to be an optional peer dependency of FLE bindings.

h3. AC
* Change the package.json to specify peer optional
* Verify it is installed in CI for testing now that it is optional

summary: Write Concern 0 Must Not Affect Read Operations
description:
When the driver has a write concern of 0 it does not apply the lsid to any initial command, including reads. This causes issues with cursors as the initial find command does not have an lsid attached but the subsequent getMore commands do. This causes an error on the server: "Cannot run getMore on cursor x, which was not created in a session...".

Steps to reproduce:
 - Create a client with writeConcern 0.
 - Insert documents into a collection more than the default batch size
 - Execute {{collection.find({}).toArray()}} to run the find and at least one getMore

The sessions spec states the lsid MUST be sent on all commands with 1 exception being unacknowledged writes - the driver is improperly treating reads the same here: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/main/src/sessions.ts#L960]

Open question: What should be the behaviour for the generic run command?

summary: Clarify behavior for runOn.minServerVersion for latest servers
Exclusively depend on existence of logicalSessionsTimeoutMinutes for sessions support
description: See DRIVERS-1230 for updated details.

summary: Clarify server selection process for change stream resume
Add tests to ensure that no BigInt features are used when useBigInt64 flag is set to false
description:
h3. Use Case

As a driver engineer
I want to add tests that ensure that js-bson works in environments that don't support BigInt
So that we can be sure that js-bson functions in restricted environments
h3. Description

The BigInt support initiative has led to a large number of significant code changes that make use of the fairly new (added in 2018) BigInt class in Javascript. We need to make sure that in environments that do not support BigInt directly, users can still rely on the existing functionality of the library and the {{Long}} class.
h3. Unknowns
 * do we need to create a new CI variant that runs these tests?
 ** Yes, as this would check all of the same code paths that we already test to catch any new or unexpected uses of BigInt APIs
 * Is it sufficient to just make use of {{node:vm}} to simulate a restricted environment?

h3. Acceptance Criteria
 * -Tests that check for prior functionality of js-bson when {{useBigInt64}} is false in an environment that does not support bigint-
 * Create eslint plugin to check for use of BigInt literals and functions
 * Add evergreen task that runs bson test suite without access to BigInt APIs

summary: endSession does not wait for abortTransaction to finish
description:
See https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/issues/451

This is particularly problematic b/c the {{abortTransaction}} call returns a Promise that we do not handle and/or return to the user, making it possible for there to be an uncaught exception.

summary: Memory leak introduced in 4.13.0
description:
h3. We experience a memory leak in the mongodb node driver introduced in version 4.13.0. The issue still persists in version 5.0.0
h3. The leak can be replicated with this setup:
[https://github.com/awilts/mongodb-leak]

Check out the repository and run {_}npm run test{_}.
h3. With driver version 4.13.0 memory consumption will grow with each iteration until it finally reaches ~80MB after 1000 iterations. 
With version 4.12.1 there is almost no growth per iteration.
h3. This leak prevents us from adopting the newer driver version. In our jest test setup we start & shutdown our app frequently. Because of this leak memory is not released after shutdown. This causes our tests to run into OOM errors.
h3. Is this a known issue? I was unable to find any related tickets.

summary: Write Concern 0 Must Not Affect Read Operations [5.x]
description:
When the driver has a write concern of 0 it does not apply the lsid to any initial command, including reads. This causes issues with cursors as the initial find command does not have an lsid attached but the subsequent getMore commands do. This causes an error on the server: "Cannot run getMore on cursor x, which was not created in a session...".

Steps to reproduce:
 - Create a client with writeConcern 0.
 - Insert documents into a collection more than the default batch size
 - Execute {{collection.find({}).toArray()}} to run the find and at least one getMore

The sessions spec states the lsid MUST be sent on all commands with 1 exception being unacknowledged writes - the driver is improperly treating reads the same here: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/main/src/sessions.ts#L960]

Open question: What should be the behaviour for the generic run command?

---

Cloned this from the original. This bug still persists in 5.x, we need a forward port

summary: High rate of reconnections due to Min Pool Size Check
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

High rate of reconnections

!image-2022-11-30-14-40-37-564.png!

 
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

NodeJS v18.12.1

MongoDB NodeJS Driver: 4.12.1
h3. Steps to reproduce?

Connection string = mongodb://mongo.common?appName=alexa_ap&family=4&connectTimeoutMS=2500&socketTimeoutMS=5000&maxPoolSize=100&minPoolSize=50&w=0&readPreference=secondaryPreferred&journal=false&noDelay=true&useUnifiedTopology=true&useNewUrlParser=true&wtimeoutMS=250&retryReads=false&retryWrites=false&readConcernLevel=available&maxIdleTimeMS=300000&serverSelectionTimeoutMS=750&minHeartbeatFrequencyMS=2000&heartbeatFrequencyMS=20000

It is possible to observe the reconnections through MongoDB logs. The reconnections starts just after the driver initiate and without any loading.

summary: Document `ObjectId` can only be constructed with `new`
description:
h3. What problem are you facing?

When upgrading to the MongoDB Node 5.0 driver, all calls to `ObjectId()` without `new` result in the following error:

```

Class constructor ObjectId cannot be invoked without 'new'

```

I couldn't find this breaking change documented anywhere, so I wasn't sure if it was actually intentional. I don't know if this was _formally_ supported, but it's relatively common use. For example, [this StackOverflow answer|https://stackoverflow.com/a/44269591/372767] discusses them being equivalent and the console docs provide [examples of constructing them without the keyword|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/method/ObjectId/] (I recognize that parity with the console is not guaranteed, but my understanding is that it is generally aimed for). Here's an [example case|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75553265/i-cant-delete-a-data-from-mongodb-objectid-class-constructor-error] of someone running into this in the community.

If this is actually intended and I missed the change notice this could be closed. If it was intended but was not documented perhaps it could be mentioned in the [upgrade guide|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/HEAD/etc/notes/CHANGES_5.0.0.md]. If it was not intended then this can serve as a bug report.

Thanks!
h3. What driver and relevant dependency versions are you using?

mongodb 5.0.1 driver
h3. Steps to reproduce?

```

import \{ ObjectId } from 'mongodb'

const x = ObjectId()

```

summary: Error message for X.509 without authSource=$external is unhelpful
description:
See COMPASS-5801 and MONGOSH-1052:

Connecting to a connection string like "mongodb+srv://hostname/?authMechanism=MONGODB-X509&tls=true&tlsCertificateKeyFile=filename" fails with "[object Object] can only have authSource set to '$external'", where obviously "[object Object]" is not what this should really say.

Source line: https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/ac4aabc9cf49c78ca5854b0185e2fc5ae983d3cf/src/connection_string.ts#L339

summary: Find operations fail when passed an ObjectId as a filter
description:
[JoCat|https://github.com/JoCat] has created [PR #3604: fix: passing of ObjectId as a filter|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/3604] in [node-mongodb-native|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native]

h3. AC
h4. Implementation

* correct the _bsontype check against 'ObjectID' to be 'ObjectId' to correctly identify ObjectId instances.

h4. Testing

* Ensure all find APIs that use the FindOperation class support the syntax of passing an ObjectId directly as a filter
* Ensure the filter always maps _id to the provided ObjectId instance

h4. Follow Up

* File a ticket in the v6 epic about the future support of this functionality, include
** Typescript definition considerations
** API Documentation, deprecation or otherwise communication
** Unsupported similar functionality, ex. lack of support in {{findAnd*}} methods
